article_meta.title: Crisis Leadership: Using Technology To Maintain Stability And GrowthByPhil Portman,Forbes Councils Member.for

transcript.content:
Summarize the following news article and list its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{"summary": "string", "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"], "sources_represented": ["string"]}

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Crisis Leadership: Using Technology To Maintain Stability And GrowthByPhil Portman,Forbes Councils Member.for
SOURCE: Forbes

ARTICLE:

InnovationCrisis Leadership: Using Technology To Maintain Stability And GrowthByPhil Portman,Forbes Councils Member.for Forbes Technology CouncilCOUNCIL POSTExpertise from Forbes Councils members, operated under license. Opinions expressed are those of the author. | Membership (fee-based)Jul 09, 2024, 08:30am EDTPhil Portman is a serial entrepreneur and the Founder & CEO of Textdrip — a small business SMS marketing tool to automate SMS campaigns. gettyWhen things are going well, leadership looks like an easy task. Right? The economy is growing, people's incomes are increasing and your organization is getting more orders.In short, all is well. So you don't need to make quick decisions.You can easily plan for the quarter, set your goals and be a true visionary leader for your colleagues, society and investors. However, the real leadership challenge arises during the crisis. Using cutting-edge technologies, you can easily survive these VUCAP times.VUCAP is an acronym where each character signifies a specific crisis condition.• V: Volatility.• U: Uncertainty.• C: Complexity.• A: Ambiguity.• P: Pandemic.The best way to deal with VUCAP times is to use automated communication tools. These tools can improve and integrate workflow management, collaboration, strategic planning and AI automation for organizational resilience.Crisis Leadership: The Cornerstone Of StabilityCrisis leadership means enabling the organization to adapt to any unexpected event and try to ease its effects by protecting the stakeholder's trust and organizational stability.
---

```json
{
  "summary": "The article discusses crisis leadership and how technology, particularly automated communication tools, can help organizations maintain stability and growth during challenging times characterized by volatility, uncertainty, complexity, ambiguity, and pandemics (VUCAP). It emphasizes that while leadership appears easy during prosperous times, the real test comes during crises, and leveraging cutting-edge technologies like workflow management, collaboration tools, and AI automation can help organizations survive and adapt.",
  "key_facts": [
    "VUCAP is an acronym representing crisis conditions: Volatility, Uncertainty, Complexity, Ambiguity, and Pandemic",

    "Technology solutions include workflow management, collaboration tools, strategic planning, and AI automation for organizational resilience",
    "Phil Portman, Founder and CEO of Textdrip, authored the article for Forbes Technology Council"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Forbes"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: The article discusses crisis leadership and how technology, particularly automated communication tools, can help organizations maintain stability and growth during challenging times characterized by volatility, uncertainty, complexity, ambiguity, and pandemics (VUCAP). It emphasizes that while leadership appears easy during prosperous times, the real test comes during crises, and leveraging cutting-edge technologies like workflow management, collaboration tools, and AI automation can help organizations survive and adapt.
key_facts: VUCAP is an acronym representing crisis conditions: Volatility, Uncertainty, Complexity, Ambiguity, and Pandemic
Crisis leadership involves enabling organizations to adapt to unexpected events while protecting stakeholder trust and organizational stability
Automated communication tools are recommended as the best way to deal with VUCAP times
Technology solutions include workflow management, collaboration tools, strategic planning, and AI automation for organizational resilience
Phil Portman, Founder and CEO of Textdrip, authored the article for Forbes Technology Council
